hypothesis: If combining pre-training on large chemical databases, domain-specific fine-tuning on CTF structures, and reinforcement learning with expert-defined physicochemical reward functions, then generative models will produce novel CTF candidates with higher validity, diversity, and compliance with structural requirements for supercapacitor applications compared to models using any single knowledge transfer strategy alone, because the sequential combination of transfer learning stages progressively constrains the chemical space toward synthetically accessible, domain-relevant structures while reward-guided optimization enforces the specific geometric and electronic properties necessary for porous carbon material functionality.
